notes.content: The Applied Mathematics major is ideal for students who are interested in mathematics used in the physical, life and social sciences, and engineering, as well as practical applications in society, including business, finance, medicine and the internet. Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major. To be considered for this major, you must complete the following by the end of spring before transfer: Four semesters/Five quarters of Calculus through Multivariable, and Either Linear Algebra or Differential Equations. All courses must be taken for a letter grade. For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process visit https:// math.ucla.edu and https:// admission.ucla.edu . PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation requirements for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.
notes.contentHtml:
The Applied Mathematics major is ideal for students who are interested in mathematics used in the physical, life and social sciences, and engineering, as well as practical applications in society, including business, finance, medicine and the internet.


Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major.


To be considered for this major, you must complete the following by the end of spring before transfer:

  • Four semesters/Five quarters of Calculus through Multivariable, and
  • Either Linear Algebra or Differential Equations.

All courses must be taken for a letter grade.


For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process visit https://math.ucla.edu and https://admission.ucla.edu.


PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation requirements for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.

notes.content: Additional recommended courses prior to transfer include: completion of the full calculus series (encompassing single variable, multivariable, linear algebra and differential equations) and one introductory course in C++.
notes.contentHtml: Additional recommended courses prior to transfer include: completion of the full calculus series (encompassing single variable, multivariable, linear algebra and differential equations) and one introductory course in C++.

notes.content: The AP Exam Scores listed below may be used to satisfy the major prep: A score of 5 on the AP Calculus AB exam will satisfy MATH 31A. A score of 4 on the AP Calculus BC exam will satisfy MATH 31A. A score of 5 on the AP Calculus BC exam will satisfy MATH 31A and 31B. The IB Exam Scores listed below may be used to satisfy the major prep: A score of 6 or higher on the IB HL Math Analysis & Approaches exam will satisfy MATH 31A.
notes.contentHtml:
The AP Exam Scores listed below may be used to satisfy the major prep:

  • A score of 5 on the AP Calculus AB exam will satisfy MATH 31A.
  • A score of 4 on the AP Calculus BC exam will satisfy MATH 31A.
  • A score of 5 on the AP Calculus BC exam will satisfy MATH 31A and 31B.


The IB Exam Scores listed below may be used to satisfy the major prep:

  • A score of 6 or higher on the IB HL Math Analysis & Approaches exam will satisfy MATH 31A.
